{{Short description|Israeli computer scientist}}
{{Infobox scientist
| name = Yonatan Sompolinsky
| nationality = Israeli
| fields = [[Cryptography]], [[Distributed computing]], [[Cryptocurrency]]
| workplaces = {{unbulleted list|[[Harvard University]] (CRCS)|[[Hebrew University of Jerusalem]]}}
| known_for = [[GHOST protocol]], PHANTOM, GHOSTDAG, DAG-KNIGHT, [[Kaspa (cryptocurrency)|Kaspa]]
| website =
}}

'''Yonatan Sompolinsky''' is an Israeli [[computer scientist]] known for his research on [[blockchain]] scalability and [[consensus mechanism|consensus protocols]]. He is the creator of the GHOST protocol, which influenced [[Ethereum]]'s fork-choice rule,<ref name="ghost">{{cite journal |last1=Sompolinsky |first1=Yonatan |last2=Zohar |first2=Aviv |title=Secure High-Rate Transaction Processing in Bitcoin |journal=Financial Cryptography and Data Security |year=2015 |doi=10.1007/978-3-662-47854-7_32}}</ref> and the founder of the [[Kaspa (cryptocurrency)|Kaspa]] cryptocurrency.<ref name="kaspa">{{cite web |url=https://kaspa.org/contributors/ |title=Kaspa Contributors |publisher=Kaspa.org |access-date=2026-03-09}}</ref>

== Career ==

Sompolinsky conducted research at the [[Hebrew University of Jerusalem]] and subsequently at [[Harvard University]]'s Center for Research on Computation and Society (CRCS).<ref name="harvard">{{cite web |url=https://projects.iq.harvard.edu/applied-cryptography-society/publications/he-dag-knight-protocol-parameterless-generalization |title=The DAG KNIGHT Protocol |publisher=Harvard CRCS |access-date=2026-03-09}}</ref>

He founded DAGLabs, a [[cryptocurrency]] research and development company, which was dissolved around the time of Kaspa's launch in November 2021, transitioning the project to community-led development.<ref name="fairlaunch">{{cite web |url=https://kaspa.org/kaspas-fair-launch/ |title=Kaspa's Fair Launch |publisher=Kaspa.org |access-date=2026-03-09}}</ref>

== Research contributions ==

=== GHOST protocol (2013/2015) ===

Sompolinsky, together with Aviv Zohar, proposed the GHOST (Greedy Heaviest Observed Sub-Tree) protocol for secure high-rate transaction processing in [[Bitcoin]]-like systems. The protocol modifies Bitcoin's longest-chain rule by weighing the entire sub-tree of blocks rather than just the longest path, improving security under high block rates.<ref name="ghost" />

The GHOST protocol was referenced in [[Ethereum]]'s design; Ethereum's fork-choice rule (LMD-GHOST, or Latest Message Driven GHOST) is a modified version adapted for [[proof-of-stake]].<ref name="ethgasper">{{cite web |url=https://ethereum.org/developers/docs/consensus-mechanisms/pos/gasper/ |title=Gasper |publisher=Ethereum.org |access-date=2026-03-09}}</ref>

=== SPECTRE (2016) ===

With Yoad Lewenberg and Aviv Zohar, Sompolinsky proposed SPECTRE (Serialization of Proof-of-work Events: Confirming Transactions via Recursive Elections), a [[Directed acyclic graph|blockDAG]] protocol that provides fast transaction confirmation through pairwise ordering rather than a total block ordering.<ref name="spectre">{{cite web |url=https://eprint.iacr.org/2016/1159 |title=SPECTRE: A Fast and Scalable Cryptocurrency Protocol |last1=Sompolinsky |first1=Yonatan |last2=Lewenberg |first2=Yoad |last3=Zohar |first3=Aviv |year=2016 |publisher=IACR ePrint}}</ref>

=== PHANTOM and GHOSTDAG (2018/2021) ===

With Shai Wyborski and Aviv Zohar, Sompolinsky proposed PHANTOM and its practical greedy approximation GHOSTDAG. The protocols generalize [[Nakamoto consensus]] from a blockchain to a blockDAG, enabling high block rates without the orphan-rate problem that limits single-chain systems. The work was published at the ACM Conference on Advances in Financial Technologies (AFT) in 2021.<ref name="phantom">{{cite conference |last1=Sompolinsky |first1=Yonatan |last2=Wyborski |first2=Shai |last3=Zohar |first3=Aviv |title=PHANTOM and GHOSTDAG: A Scalable Generalization of Nakamoto Consensus |conference=ACM Conference on Advances in Financial Technologies (AFT 2021) |pages=57–70 |year=2021 |doi=10.1145/3479722.3480990 |url=https://dl.acm.org/doi/10.1145/3479722.3480990}}</ref>

GHOSTDAG is the consensus protocol deployed in the [[Kaspa (cryptocurrency)|Kaspa]] cryptocurrency.<ref name="kaspa" />

=== DAG-KNIGHT (2022) ===

With Michael Sutton, Sompolinsky proposed DAG-KNIGHT, described as the first permissionless [[proof-of-work]] protocol with no a priori in-protocol bound on network latency. The protocol infers network conditions from the DAG topology rather than requiring pre-configured parameters.<ref name="dagknight">{{cite web |url=https://eprint.iacr.org/2022/1494 |title=The DAG KNIGHT Protocol: A Parameterless Generalization of Nakamoto Consensus |last1=Sompolinsky |first1=Yonatan |last2=Sutton |first2=Michael |year=2022 |publisher=IACR ePrint}}</ref> The paper was published through Harvard's Center for Research on Computation and Society.<ref name="harvard" />

== Selected publications ==

* Sompolinsky, Y.; Zohar, A. (2015). "Secure High-Rate Transaction Processing in Bitcoin". ''Financial Cryptography and Data Security''. [https://doi.org/10.1007/978-3-662-47854-7_32 DOI].
* Sompolinsky, Y.; Lewenberg, Y.; Zohar, A. (2016). "SPECTRE: A Fast and Scalable Cryptocurrency Protocol". IACR ePrint. [https://eprint.iacr.org/2016/1159 ePrint 2016/1159].
* Sompolinsky, Y.; Wyborski, S.; Zohar, A. (2021). "PHANTOM and GHOSTDAG: A Scalable Generalization of Nakamoto Consensus". ''ACM Conference on Advances in Financial Technologies (AFT 2021)'', pp. 57–70. [https://dl.acm.org/doi/10.1145/3479722.3480990 DOI].
* Sompolinsky, Y.; Sutton, M. (2022). "The DAG KNIGHT Protocol: A Parameterless Generalization of Nakamoto Consensus". IACR ePrint. [https://eprint.iacr.org/2022/1494 ePrint 2022/1494].

== See also ==
* [[Kaspa (cryptocurrency)]]
* [[BlockDAG]]
* [[Blockchain]]
* [[Proof of work]]
* [[Nakamoto consensus]]

== References ==
{{Reflist}}

[[Category:Israeli computer scientists]]
[[Category:Cryptographers]]
[[Category:Hebrew University of Jerusalem alumni]]
[[Category:Harvard University people]]
[[Category:Cryptocurrency people]]
[[Category:Living people]]
